activities.title: Hawai'i Keiki Museum
activities.notes: Queens' Marketplace. $10/kid $15/adult. Free re-entry within 7 days.
activities.location: Queens' MarketPlace, 69-201 Waikoloa Beach Dr, Waikoloa Village, HI 96738

activities.title: Anchialine Ponds (WAPPA)
activities.notes: Brackish lava pools, ʻōpaeʻula shrimp. Short mostly-flat walk on uneven lava rock. Sturdy sandals or closed-toe shoes recommended. Stroller: partial — manageable on a sturdy all-terrain stroller, but lava surface is rough. Trail end near beach may flood at high tide. DO NOT enter ponds, throw objects, disturb rocks — UH research preserve, rules enforced. Nene and feral cats present — don't feed. Best at sunset: ponds reflect colors beautifully. Free, no booking.
activities.location: Waikoloa Anchialine Pond Preservation Area, Waikoloa Beach Dr, Waikoloa, HI 96738

activities.title: King's Trail (Ala Kahakai)
activities.notes: Ancient coastal lava rock trail — uneven, rough surface. NO STROLLER on the trail itself. Use carrier for Heiki. Sturdy closed-toe shoes required. Ponds viewing area nearby has flat paved path — stroller OK there. DO NOT walk on or remove rocks from historic walls. Bring water and sunscreen — exposed coast, no shade. Ancient fishponds (Kuʻualiʻi and Kahapapa) en route — do not enter the water or fish. Parking lot open 7am–8pm. Best early morning or sunset.
activities.location: Anaeho'omalu Bay, 69-275 Waikoloa Beach Dr, Waikoloa, HI 96738

activities.title: Waikoloa Petroglyph Reserve
activities.notes: Free guided tour Thu–Sun 9:30am — start near Kings' Shops gas station, trail to the right. ~0.5 mile round trip, 30–60 min. NO STROLLER — lava surface is uneven and rough throughout, even on marked path. Closed-toe sturdy shoes required — sharp lava rock. NO SHADE WHATSOEVER — sun protection essential (hat, sunscreen, water). DO NOT touch or make rubbings of carvings — destroys centuries-old art. Best light for seeing carvings: early morning or late afternoon (slanting light). Contains thousands of carvings incl. rare horse+warrior post-contact petroglyph. Connects to King's Trail running north/south along coast. Free, no booking.
activities.location: Kings' Shops, 250 Waikoloa Beach Dr, Waikoloa Village, HI 96738

activities.title: Cheer squad — Lavaman Triathlon
activities.notes: Wife + FILs racing — finish line at A-Bay. Live music, beach BBQ. HEAT: stay in shade near water's edge, have a cold drink ready. 30 min max outdoors in direct sun — retreat to car AC if Heiki gets fussy. Sandals fine. Stroller OK on flat sand. Bring water + snacks for Heiki.
activities.location: Anaeho'omalu Bay, 69-275 Waikoloa Beach Dr, Waikoloa, HI 96738

activities.title: A-Bay afternoon
activities.notes: Keep it short and shady — pond walkway is flat and stroller-friendly, brief loop only. HEAT: 30 min max outside. Watch for sudden mood drop (first sign of overheating). Have a cold smoothie or drink ready in the car for the ride home. Skip King's Trail today — too exposed. Ponds walkway + beach shade only. If Kona seems tired or cranky before 30 min, head home — pool is the better option.
activities.location: Anaeho'omalu Bay, 69-275 Waikoloa Beach Dr, Waikoloa, HI 96738

activities.title: Pool / cool-down at home
activities.notes: Recovery after morning heat. Stay home, backyard or community pool. Keep it easy.
